title: Non-flags enums should be avoided in bitwise operations
description: Non-flags enums should not be used in bitwise operationsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellQuick FixIDE quick fixes available with SonarQube for IDEconventionWhy is this an issue?More InfoEnumerationsare commonly used to identify distinct elements from a set of values.However, they can also serve asbit flags, enabling bitwise operations to combine multiple elements within a single value.// Saturday = 0b00100000, Sunday = 0b01000000, weekend = 0b01100000 var weekend = Days.Saturday | Days.Sunday; // Combining elementsWhen enumerations are used as bit flags, it is consideredgood practiceto annotate the enum type with theFlagsAttribute:enum Permissions { None = 0, Read = 1, Write = 2, Execute = 4 } // ... var x = Permissions.Read | Permissions.Write; // Noncomplia
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
enum Permissions  // Noncompliant - no [Flags] attribute but used in bitwise ops
{
    None = 0,
    Read = 1,
    Write = 2,
    Execute = 4
}

enum Days
{
    Monday = 1,
    Tuesday = 2
}

var readWrite = Permissions.Read | Permissions.Write;  // Noncompliant
var weekdays = Days.Monday | Days.Tuesday;  // Noncompliant
compliant:
[Flags]
enum Permissions  // Compliant - has [Flags] attribute
{
    None = 0,
    Read = 1,
    Write = 2,
    Execute = 4,
    All = Read | Write | Execute
}

enum Days  // Compliant - not used in bitwise operations
{
    Monday = 1,
    Tuesday = 2
}

var readWrite = Permissions.Read | Permissions.Write;  // Compliant
var today = Days.Monday;  // Compliant - no bitwise operation

checks.description: Check for bitwise operations on enums that don't have [Flags] attribute

logic.triggering_conditions: MethodDeclaration nodes containing bitwise operations on enum values where the enum type lacks [Flags] attribute
exclusion_conditions: Bitwise operations on non-enum types (int, byte, etc.)
never_trigger_on: Bitwise operations on non-enum types (int, byte, etc.)
logic.reasoning: Detects bitwise operations (|, &, ^) on enum values in method bodies. Uses MethodDeclaration nodes to focus on actual code rather than enum declarations. The pattern looks for Type.Member | Type.Member syntax. This approach avoids the complexity of correlating enum declarations with [Flags] attributes by focusing on method-level violations where bitwise operations occur.

should_not_trigger: var single = Days.Monday; // no bitwise operation
